title: poa alpina subsp. parnassica
intro: Poa alpina subsp. parnassica, also known as Alpine Meadow-grass, is a subspecies of the Alpine grass, Poa alpina, which is a type of flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Greece. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Poa alpina subsp. parnassica include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into infusions or decoctions, which can be consumed as teas or used topically to reap its benefits, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
